generatedFrom.note: No HTML prototype in spec/ — access patterns below are INFERRED from the obvious todos UI (a list screen of todos, a lists sidebar, toggle/archive by id). Replace source='inferred' with real prototype regions once a spec/todos.html exists; the store calls may shift.

entities.name: todo
entities.purpose: A single task inside a list — added, then completed, then archived. Its done-state is just the latest entry in its own history.

usedBy.does: creates the todo (seeds its first 'added' state)
lists & filters a list's todos (active / done)

entities.rationale: A todo is read in the list screen (a list's todos, filtered active/done) — that's a query, so Firestore. The only by-id reads are the in-request load() during complete/discard, served by a fast KV copy.

accessPatterns.operation: list a list's todos, filter active/done, ordered
accessPatterns.source: inferred (no prototype): the main todos screen

accessPatterns.operation: load one todo by id to toggle done / archive it
append a state change (completed / archived) to a todo

projections.name: todo:byId
projections.why: the load(id) inside complete/discard is a known-key point lookup on the hot write path — a KV get is sub-ms and needs no index; mirror on every save.

indexes.for: todo list screen: a list's todos, filtered active/done

immutability.mutationFound: spec todo.complete and todo.discard both do db:todo.load(id) -> mutate(done/archive) -> db:todo.save: in-place edits that overwrite the todo's prior state.
immutability.currentStateOnRead: todo.done and whether it is archived derive from the last element of states[]; todo.add seeds states[0] (kind=added, done=false), todo.complete appends (kind=completed, done=true), todo.discard appends (kind=archived).
immutability.realization: embedded array on the todo document — a todo has only a handful of state changes and they are always read with the todo. The todo id is stable; only states accrete, so completing then reopening is a full trail, never an overwrite.

entities.name: list
entities.purpose: A named bucket that groups todos. Created once; each todo points back to it by listId.
entities.rationale: Lists show up as a small set in the sidebar — a tiny query, which Firestore serves cheaply. No hot by-id lookup or counter pushes it toward KV.

accessPatterns.operation: show all of a user's lists (sidebar)

indexes.for: lists sidebar: a user's lists, newest first

immutability.mutationFound: none — list.create is id::generate -> [NEW] list -> fill -> db:list.save. No db:list.load, so no load->mutate->save against a persisted list; the fresh id is written once and never edited.
immutability.realization: single immutable document per created list id

notes: todo.add and list.create are fresh-id saves — already append-friendly; todo.add seeds the immutable states[] with the first (kind=added) entry.

todo.complete and todo.discard are the in-place edits the scan flagged — restructured from load->mutate->save into appended state entries, so a todo's history (added -> completed -> archived) is never overwritten.
ASSUMPTION: store calls for the two 'inferred' read patterns should be re-checked against a real spec/todos.html prototype; the spec alone does not reveal how todos are queried.
listId / ownerId / createdAt are implied by the read patterns but absent from TodoDto/ListDto — add them via rune:spec before these queries/indexes can be built.
